---
title: "EDA 62 — Predisposing Variable Profile: Socio-demographics"
subtitle: "Andersen Behavioral Model — Predisposing Domain"
author: "Analysis Team"
date: "2026-05-22"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    toc: true
    toc-location: right
    code-fold: true
    theme: yeti
    highlight-style: breeze
    code-line-numbers: true
    self-contained: true
    embed-resources: true
    keep-md: false
editor: visual
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---

# Mission

This EDA profiles the 11 predisposing variables (socio-demographic background) from the
Andersen Behavioral Model framework. These represent immutable or slowly-changing
characteristics that predispose individuals toward or away from health service use and,
by extension, work absence.

**Research Questions:**

1. What is the weighted distribution of each predisposing variable in the employed population?
2. How does each predisposing variable relate to the primary outcome (`days_absent_total`) —
   mean days, median days, and zero-proportion?
3. What is the missing-data pattern across the 11 predisposing variables?
4. What demographic interactions exist — age×sex, education×immigration cross-tabs?

Following the dual-file development pattern, all analytical code lives in
`analysis/eda-62/eda-62.R`. This document sources those chunks for presentation.

# Definition of Terms

**Predisposing factors**: In the Andersen Behavioral Model, these are characteristics
that exist prior to the onset of illness — demographics (age, sex), social structure
(education, marital status, immigration), and beliefs. They predispose individuals to
differential health service use.

**`days_absent_total`**: Primary outcome. Row-wise sum of all 8 LOP component variables,
representing total workdays missed in the past 3 months due to health reasons.

```{r}
#| code-summary: Reconciles working directory between execution modes
#| echo: false
#| results: hide
#| message: false
#| warning: false
cat("Working directory: ", getwd())
library(knitr)
opts_knit$set(root.dir = "../../")
```

```{r}
#| label: set_options
#| code-summary: Report-wide formatting options
#| echo: false
#| results: hide
#| message: false
cat("Working directory: ", getwd())
report_render_start_time <- Sys.time()
opts_chunk$set(
  results   = "show",
  message   = FALSE,
  warning   = FALSE,
  comment   = NA,
  tidy      = FALSE,
  out.width = "960px",
  fig.width  = 10,
  fig.height = 6,
  fig.path   = "figure-png-iso/"
)
options(width = 100)
ggplot2::theme_set(ggplot2::theme_minimal())
read_chunk("analysis/eda-62/eda-62.R")
```

# Environment

> Reviews the components of the working environment. Non-technical readers may skip this section.

```{r}
#| label: load-packages
#| code-summary: Packages used in the current report
#| echo: true
#| results: hide
#| message: false
#| code-fold: true
```

```{r}
#| label: load-sources
#| code-summary: Collection of custom functions used in current repository
#| echo: true
#| results: hide
#| message: false
#| code-fold: true
```

```{r}
#| label: declare-globals
#| code-summary: Values used throughout the report
#| echo: true
#| results: hide
#| message: false
#| code-fold: true
```

```{r}
#| label: declare-functions
#| code-summary: Custom functions defined for use in this report
#| echo: true
#| results: hide
#| message: false
#| code-fold: true
```

# Data

```{r}
#| label: load-data
#| code-summary: Load data from Ellis parquet outputs
#| warning: false
#| code-fold: true
#| cache: true
```

## Data Context

For complete data documentation, see the
[Data Primer](../data-primer-1/data-primer-1.html).

```{r}
#| label: data-context-tables
#| code-summary: Tables and variables used in this analysis
#| code-fold: true
```

```{r}
#| label: data-context-person
#| code-summary: Representative individual
#| code-fold: true
```

```{r}
#| label: data-context-distributions
#| code-summary: Level counts for predisposing variables
#| code-fold: true
```

```{r}
#| label: tweak-data-0
#| code-summary: Ensure factor types for categorical variables
#| code-fold: true
#| cache: true
```

```{r}
#| label: inspect-data-0
#| code-summary: Data overview
#| code-fold: true
```

# Analysis

## Graph Family G1: Variable Distributions

<!-- Research question: What is the weighted distribution of each predisposing variable? -->

```{r}
#| label: g1-data-prep
#| code-summary: Prepare distribution summaries
#| code-fold: true
#| cache: true
```

```{r}
#| label: g1
#| code-summary: Unweighted distribution of predisposing variables
#| code-fold: true
#| fig-cap: "Unweighted frequency distribution by variable level"
#| fig-height: 8
```

```{r}
#| label: g11
#| code-summary: Weighted distribution of predisposing variables
#| code-fold: true
#| fig-cap: "Population-weighted distribution using wts_m_pooled"
#| fig-height: 8
```

```{r}
#| label: g12
#| code-summary: Weighted distribution by CCHS cycle
#| code-fold: true
#| fig-cap: "Distribution comparison between 2010-2011 and 2013-2014 cycles"
#| fig-height: 8
```

## Graph Family G2: Outcome Relationship

<!-- Research question: How does each predisposing variable relate to days_absent_total? -->

```{r}
#| label: g2-data-prep
#| code-summary: Prepare outcome summaries by variable level
#| code-fold: true
#| cache: true
```

```{r}
#| label: g2
#| code-summary: Mean days absent by predisposing variable level
#| code-fold: true
#| fig-cap: "Weighted mean days absent by demographic level"
#| fig-height: 8
```

```{r}
#| label: g21
#| code-summary: Median days absent (IQR) by variable level
#| code-fold: true
#| fig-cap: "Median and interquartile range of days absent"
#| fig-height: 8
```

```{r}
#| label: g22
#| code-summary: Zero-day proportion by variable level
#| code-fold: true
#| fig-cap: "Proportion reporting zero days absent by demographic group"
#| fig-height: 8
```

## Graph Family G3: Missing Data Profile

<!-- Research question: What is the missing-data pattern across predisposing variables? -->

```{r}
#| label: g3-data-prep
#| code-summary: Compute missingness statistics
#| code-fold: true
#| cache: true
```

```{r}
#| label: g3
#| code-summary: Missingness profile across predisposing variables
#| code-fold: true
#| fig-cap: "Percentage and count of missing values per variable"
```

```{r}
#| label: g31
#| code-summary: Missingness by CCHS cycle
#| code-fold: true
#| fig-cap: "Missing data rates by survey cycle"
```

## Graph Family G4: Demographic Interactions

<!-- Research question: What demographic interactions exist? -->

```{r}
#| label: g4-data-prep
#| code-summary: Compute cross-tabulated outcome summaries
#| code-fold: true
#| cache: true
```

```{r}
#| label: g4
#| code-summary: Age group × sex interaction
#| code-fold: true
#| fig-cap: "Mean days absent by age group and sex"
```

```{r}
#| label: g41
#| code-summary: Education × immigration status interaction
#| code-fold: true
#| fig-cap: "Mean days absent by education level and immigration status"
```
